title: Entry Identity, Idempotent Upserts, Version History, and Tombstones · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds versioned idempotent entry repository from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned idempotent entry repository while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Scope external IDs to feed and format semantics, detect collisions, and preserve the raw identity basis and parser version.
Return explicit inserted, unchanged, revised, conflicting, quarantined, or rejected results from atomic constraints and comparison.
Store bounded meaningful revisions with field diff, fetch identity, timestamps, and correction or moderation cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Scoped Entry Key
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 1077 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove scoped identity, typed upsert outcomes, revisions, tombstones, and replay convergence; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Scoped Entry Key.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for scope external ids to feed and format semantics, detect collisions, and preserve the raw identity basis and parser version. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Scoped Entry Key, keep Feed Ts Scoped Entry Key connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
